schema.description: A server for querying car pricing information from the FIPE API, including brand listings, model searches, and vehicle type filtering.

tools.name: get_car_brands
tools.description: Retrieve all available car brands from the FIPE API with optional result limiting.

limit.description: Maximum number of brand results to return. Defaults to 10 if not specified.

brands.description: List of car brands available in the FIPE database.

brand_code.description: The unique numeric identifier for the car brand in the FIPE system.

brand_name.description: The display name of the car brand (e.g., 'Toyota', 'Honda', 'Ford').

tools.name: search_car_price
tools.description: Search for car models and their current market prices by brand name.

brand_name.description: The display name of the car brand to search for (e.g., 'Toyota', 'Honda', 'Ford').

vehicles.description: List of vehicle models matching the specified brand with pricing information.

model_name.description: The full model name of the vehicle.

production_year.description: The year the vehicle was manufactured.

fuel_type.description: The type of fuel the vehicle uses (e.g., 'Gasoline', 'Diesel', 'Electric', 'Flex').

current_price.description: The current market price of the vehicle in Brazilian Reais (BRL) according to FIPE.

fipe_code.description: The unique FIPE reference code for this specific vehicle configuration.

tools.name: get_vehicles_by_type
tools.description: Retrieve available vehicle brands filtered by vehicle type category.

vehicle_type.description: The category of vehicles to retrieve. Accepts 'carros' or 'cars' for automobiles, 'motos' or 'motorcycles' for two-wheelers, 'caminhoes' or 'trucks' for commercial trucks.

brands.description: List of brands available for the specified vehicle type in the FIPE database.

brand_code.description: The unique numeric identifier for the car brand in the FIPE system.

brand_name.description: The display name of the car brand (e.g., 'Toyota', 'Honda', 'Ford').

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with one brand and two vehicles - tests minimal data structure
Multiple brands with varied vehicle types and fuel options - tests typical use case with cars and motorcycles
Comprehensive scenario with multiple brands, diverse vehicle types (cars, trucks, motorcycles), various fuel types, and different production years - tests full functionality
Edge case with empty collections and extreme values - tests handling of minimal data and boundary conditions

Fiat.model_name: Concept Car Future Model with Very Long Name to Test String Length Handling

test_cases.output: Returns 1 brand (Toyota) as expected from loaded scenario
Returns 1 brand (Toyota) with default limit behavior
Returns 2 vehicles (Corolla and Hilux) with complete pricing information
Returns empty vehicles list as expected
Returns empty brands list - potential issue with vehicle_types lookup logic
Returns empty brands list - potential issue with vehicle_types lookup logic
Returns empty brands list as expected (no motorcycles in scenario)

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly. All fields preserved correctly.

errors.error_details: The tool returns empty brand lists ({"brands": []}) for all tested vehicle types: 'cars', 'carros', 'motorcycles', 'motos'. The scenario contains vehicle_types data with 2 car brands (Honda, Volkswagen) and 2 motorcycle brands (Honda, Yamaha).
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: For 'cars'/'carros' - should return Honda and Volkswagen brands. For 'motorcycles'/'motos' - should return Honda and Yamaha brands. Actual: Empty list for all vehicle types.
errors.root_cause: The get_vehicles_by_type method in the API is not correctly accessing or mapping the vehicle_types data from the loaded scenario. Possible causes: (1) Incorrect key mapping between input parameter and scenario data structure, (2) The API method is not reading from the correct state variable, (3) Type normalization logic may not be working correctly to map 'cars' to 'cars' or 'carros' to 'cars' in the scenario data.

tool_executions.error: Tool returns empty brand lists for all vehicle types when data exists in scenario

errors.error_details: The get_vehicles_by_type tool consistently returns empty brands list for all vehicle type inputs ('cars', 'carros', 'motorcycles', 'trucks'), despite the scenario containing a properly populated vehicle_types dictionary with mappings for all these categories.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: For 'cars' input, should return 4 brands [Ford, Chevrolet, BMW, Tesla]. For 'motorcycles', should return 1 brand [Harley-Davidson]. For 'trucks', should return 2 brands [Ford, Chevrolet]. Actual: All queries return empty brands list: {'brands': []}
errors.root_cause: The get_vehicles_by_type implementation in the API layer is not correctly accessing or querying the vehicle_types data structure from the loaded scenario. Possible issues: (1) The tool may not be properly mapping input parameters to the scenario's vehicle_types keys, (2) The API method may not be reading from the correct state variable, or (3) There may be a key normalization issue between input vehicle types and the stored keys in vehicle_types dictionary.

test_cases.test_name: Boundary case - limit exceeds available brands

test_cases.output: Returns 3 Ford vehicles with correct details
Returns 2 Tesla vehicles with correct details
Returns 2 BMW vehicles with correct details

Expected 4 car brands but got empty list
Expected 1 motorcycle brand (Harley-Davidson) but got empty list
Expected 2 truck brands (Ford, Chevrolet) but got empty list

tool_executions.error: Tool returns empty results for all vehicle types despite scenario data containing vehicle_types with proper mappings

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly - all brands, vehicles, and vehicle_types preserved correctly
